name: White Christmas Cake ~
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop up the White Chocolate Baking Bars (8 ounces total), reserving half for the frosting.
2. In a large saucepan, bring margarine or butter and 3/4 cup water to boiling, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Add baking bar, stirring until melted. Stir in buttermilk, eggs, and rum extract; set aside.
3. Stir together 1/2 cup of the flour and pecans; set aside.
4. In an extra-large bowl, stir together the remaining 3 cups flour, sugar, coconut, baking soda, and baking powder. Add the margarine/butter mixture; beat in the pecan mixture. Fold in the chocolate mixture. Pour into 3 greased and floured 9 x 1 1/2-inch round baking pans.
5. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 25 to 30 minutes, or until done. Cool in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans and complete cooling on wire racks.
6. To prepare the frosting, melt the reserved White Chocolate Baking Bar. Cool for 10 minutes.
7. In a large mixing bowl, beat softened margarine/butter (11 ounces) and cream cheese until combined. Beat in chocolate. Gradually add confectioners sugar, beating until smooth.
8. Assemble by placing a cake layer on a platter. Spread with 1/2 cup frosting. Repeat layers. Top with remaining cake layer. Frost top and sides with remaining frosting. Garnish by sprinkling toasted coconut in a large circle on top of the cake.
